results.title: Farewell Oak Street
results.creator_credit: Grant McLean, Gordon Burwash and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: A dramatised story of what a low-rental housing project can mean to the people who once shared aged, sub-standard dwellings. A before-and-after picture about people in a large-scale public housing project in Toronto, who, because of the housing shortage, had been forced to live in squalid, dingy flats, all-purpose rooms, and ramshackle houses on a crowded street in Regent Park North. These people were freed through housing development to inhabit new, modern dwellings planned to give a measure of privacy, light and air.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Grant McLean, Gordon Burwash and National Film Board of Canada

results.title: The Family: life styles of the future (TEFC)
results.creator_credit: Beryl Fox, Douglas Leiterman, Philip S. Hobel, Document Associates and Hobel-Leiterman Productions
results.brief_description: Dr. Margaret Meade, considers the concept of the family. Whilst the nuclear family has predominated in industrial societies, Dr. Meade highlights current developments, including the communal family and the “three-way marriage”. She then progresses into a discussion on the factors that will influence the composition of the family of the future.

results.title: Family house
results.creator_credit: Laurence Hyde and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: An artistic treatment of what constitutes a change in the philosophy of family housing. Made from still photographs and nostalgic Victorian paintings, and based on an essay by the distinguished Canadian architect Humphrey Carver, the film contrasts the traditional romantic image of the home as domicile from cradle to grave with the needs of today’s more transient urban population. The film is designed to stimulate thought and discussion about housing and town planning.

results.title: Family Group
results.creator_credit: Rodney Musch, Norman Laird and Dept of Film Production for the Dept of Agriculture
results.brief_description: A sheep dog eats part of a dead sheep and spreads hydatid infection to a farmer’s family. Stresses need for constant vigilance against hydatids.

results.title: Failing to learn, learning to fail (TEFC)
results.creator_credit: Mike Gavin, Films Incorporated and National Broadcasting Company
results.brief_description: Investigates the relationship between learning disabilities and delinquent behaviour by examining the history of four youngsters afflicted with minimal brain dysfunction. Points out the importance of early diagnosis and reports on the progress being made in the treatment of this disorder.

results.title: Facts about projection
results.creator_credit: Robert Edmonds, Wesley Greene and John Ott Pictures
results.brief_description: Explains how a projectionist can put on a professional 16mm sound film screening by observing a check list of hints and safeguards. Emphasises the importance of a pre-showing check of the kind and source of power, light control facilities and ventilation; of the correct set-up of projector, speaker and screen; placement and plugging of cords; adjustment of focus, screen size, tone and volume controls and a final check for clean aperture before threading.

results.title: Faces in the sun
results.creator_credit: Cecil Holmes, Richard Mason and Methodist Overseas Mission
results.brief_description: The story of four aboriginals grappling with assimilation pressures and facing a changing future. Through the experience of a bark painter, a teaching assistant, a carpenter, and a tribal headman, we see the type of life each one lives, and the conflict of the old and the new which sets up tensions within each one, and in Aboriginal society. (Award: Gold Award, General Category, Australian Film Awards, 1965).

results.title: The Face of Lincoln
results.creator_credit: Edward Freed, Wilbur T. Blume and University of Southern California
results.brief_description: Professor Merrell Gage, head of the Department of Sculpture at the University of Southern California, describes the life and career of Abraham Lincoln as he sculptures from clay a life-like bust of the President, making physical changes in the subject’s face, hair, beard and expression to correspond with events in Lincoln’s life.

results.title: The Only way out
results.brief_description: A study of decentralisation - Australia is already one of the most highly urbanised countries in the world, and municipal and State authorities are finding it difficult to provide the facilities necessary for large populations. Examines moves towards large-scale decentralisation in South Australia and Albury/Wodonga.

results.title: F.A. Cup Final 1962
results.brief_description: Records the 1962 FA Cup Final played at Wembley Stadium between the Tottenham Hotspurs and Burnley. Use of slow motion camera work helps make this an outstanding record of a thrilling final as it follows Burnley’s attempt to stand up to the Spurs team at the peak of their form. The Queen and Prince Phillip were among the thousands of spectators.
